question_text: How does the angle at which the Sun's rays hit the Earth's surface change between summer and winter?

options.label: A
options.text: The rays are the same angle all year

options.label: B
options.text: In summer, rays hit at a steeper angle; in winter, they hit at a shallower angle

options.label: C
options.text: In summer, rays hit at a shallower angle; in winter, they hit at a steeper angle

options.label: D
options.text: The angle changes randomly throughout the year

explanation: When the Sun is high in the sky (summer), its rays hit Earth at a steeper angle, concentrating energy over a smaller area, making it warmer. In winter, the Sun is lower, rays hit at a shallower angle, and energy is spread over a larger area.
